exemplar.title: How successful is ZnO as a photocatalyst at removing C13H18O2, Ibuprofen from wastewater, with the greatest efficiency, within the range?

detailedExemplarData.title: How successful is ZnO as a photocatalyst at removing C13H18O2, Ibuprofen from wastewater, with the greatest efficiency, within the range?

strengths: Introduction is clear, logically organized and maintains a focused purpose on photocatalytic removal of ibuprofen.
Research question is explicitly stated, chemically framed and consistently referenced throughout.
Experimental plan is mostly complete with defined variables, control tables, procedure and error treatment.
weaknesses: Occasional digressions (e.g., extended local context) dilute the core focus.
Several control-table entries are blank and no replicates were run, weakening methodological rigor.
Some methodological details (pH monitoring, stirring standardization) are insufficiently specified.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a clear purpose, a well-focused research question and a mostly complete methodology. However, incomplete tables, lack of replicates and missing procedural details limit the methodological rigor, placing it in the mid-band rather than top.

strengths: Demonstrates sound chemical understanding (band-gap reasoning, BeerâLambert calculations, radical mechanisms).
Terminology is used accurately and consistently (e.g., molar absorptivity, electronâhole recombination).
Quantitative literature data are effectively integrated to contextualize the study.
weaknesses: Reliance on non-academic sources (ChatGPT, CBC) undermines academic rigor.
Minor chemical misconceptions (e.g., ZnO polarity) need correction.
In-text citation practice is inconsistent across the report.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall knowledge is clear and structured, with effective use of terminology and relevant quantitative data. The presence of secondary sources, occasional inaccuracies and inconsistent referencing limit this to a mid-band performance.

strengths: Data collection across multiple molar ratios is well planned, and UV-vis measurements are quantitative and relevant.
Analysis links observed trends to surface area, shielding and agglomeration with coherent reasoning.
Evaluation acknowledges limitations (filtration errors, heat effects) and proposes sensible improvements and future work.
weaknesses: No experimental replicates and missing data entries reduce the validity of conclusions.
Statistical analysis (regression, error propagation) is absent, limiting depth.
Some evaluative points (e.g., filter carry-over) remain speculative without proposed validation tests.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student presents good, relevant research with clear analysis and supported conclusions. Critical evaluation is present but not fully rigorous, placing the work in the mid-range of Critical Thinking.

strengths: Follows the prescribed extended essay structure with a detailed table of contents and logical section flow.
Essential layout elements (title, word count, headings, figures and tables) are present and mostly consistent.
Use of captions and numbering demonstrates attention to standard conventions.
weaknesses: Some pagination is missing or inconsistent, hindering navigation.
Formatting glitches (broken tables, placeholder text) and duplicated bibliography entries appear.
Error bars are mentioned but not displayed on graphs, reducing clarity of data presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is generally appropriate, with correct structure and layout conventions observed. However, formatting inconsistencies and missing elements prevent a flawless execution, placing it solidly in the mid-band.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows the prescribed EE structure with a detailed table of contents, numbered sections and logical flow from introduction to conclusion.  Nevertheless, missing data rows, duplicated bibliography entries and occasional formatting glitches (broken tables, placeholder text) indicate only âgenerally appropriateâ rather than flawless structure.

The student introduces the issue of pharmaceutical contamination and photocatalytic removal with ZnO in a concise, logically organised introduction.  The central concepts of photocatalysis, electronâhole generation and radical attack are described with chemical accuracy and are revisited throughout, demonstrating a clear, purposeful focus.  Occasional digressions (e.g. lengthy local context about Toronto) do not obscure the main line of inquiry, so the overall communication remains effective and aligned with the stated purpose.

The student interprets the concave trend, links it to surface area, shielding and agglomeration, and quantifies efficiency per mole of ZnO.  Limitations (filter carry-over, heat from lamp) are noted.  Statistical depth is limited (no regression statistics, standard deviations), but conclusions are generally supported by data, representing clear analysis with well-supported reasoning.

Fifteen data points across a rational molar-ratio range, quantitative UV-vis measurements, error estimates and a graph indicate good, mostly relevant research.  The absence of replicates, blank entries in data tables and reliance on a single light source limit completeness, so the strand aligns best with the upper half of the 4â6 band rather than the excellent bands.

The student demonstrates sound chemical understanding: bandâgap reasoning for ZnO, BeerâLambert calculations, radical mechanisms and catalyst agglomeration are all explained coherently.  Minor misconceptions (e.g. classifying ZnO as non-polar) and the absence of deeper kinetic treatment prevent a higher award, but knowledge is overall clear, structured and evidence-based.

Terminology such as âelectronâhole recombination rateâ, âmolar absorptivityâ, âphotocatalyst poisoningâ and error types (absolute, multiplied, relative) is used accurately and consistently.  Occasional slips in syntax and unit notation are present but do not impede clarity, supporting a solid rather than exemplary performance.

A full experimental plan is provided: independent/dependent variables, extensive control tables, step-by-step procedure, safety section and error treatment.  Nonetheless several tables are incomplete, no replicates are run, and filtration problems acknowledged in the evaluation reveal weaknesses in practical design.  Source selection is present but patchy (two ChatGPT citations, limited peer-reviewed chemistry papers).  These shortcomings lower the work from the top band but still represent a mostly complete, relevant methodology.

Essential layout featuresâtitle, word count, section headings, figures, tablesâare present and mostly consistent.  Page numbering appears in the TOC but is not visible on all pages in the extract, and figure/table captions are sometimes incomplete, so layout is correct in most but not all particulars.

The evaluation recognises methodological flaws (non-uniform irradiation, lack of trials, filtration errors) and proposes sensible improvements (UV reactor, longer runs).  Discussion of economic feasibility and future studies shows reflection, but critical weighing of alternative interpretations and rigorous error propagation are limited, placing the work at the lower end of the 4â6 band.

Key literature (photocatalysis reviews, environmental reports) is cited where concepts are introduced, and data such as molar absorptivities are applied in calculations.  However, several references are secondary or non-academic (OpenAI, CBC article), and in-text citation practice is inconsistent.  Application therefore remains largely relevant but only partially appropriate, warranting the mid-band score.

The research question â âHow successful is ZnO as a photocatalyst at removing ibuprofenâ¦?â â is explicitly stated, chemically framed (molar ratios, efficiency definition) and consistently referenced in the methodology, data collection and evaluation.  Although the wording is slightly verbose, it is sufficiently narrow (single pollutant, single catalyst, defined efficiency metric) to guide a focused investigation, fulfilling the criteria of a well-focused and effectively connected question.

annotations.comment: Suggest refining the research question by removing the ambiguous phrase âwithin the range?â to improve clarity and precision of focus.
The extensive local context on Toronto, while informative, could be condensed to maintain focus on the core chemical issue and strengthen the introductionâs relevance.
The claim that ZnO is nonpolar and insoluble in water is chemically inaccurate; clarify ZnOâs ionic nature and solubility behavior to ensure subject accuracy.
The explanation of molar ratio limits is conceptually sound but overly verbose; streamline the rationale to enhance clarity and maintain methodological focus.
A control trial without ZnO is dismissed but could serve to verify baseline photolytic effects; consider including a blank trial to confirm no degradation occurs without catalyst.
Clearly defined efficiency metric and use of BeerâLambert law show strong alignment between the research question and analytical approach.
Several cells in the controlled-variable table are blank; complete all entries to fully document controls and strengthen methodological rigor.
The student presents a clear and correctly formatted title page including word count, demonstrating strong adherence to IB formatting requirements.
Accurate word count within the 4000-word limit is clearly displayed on the title page, fulfilling IB formatting criteria.
Table of contents is comprehensive but several entries lack page numbers; ensure all sections have complete pagination for easier navigation.
Reliance on ChatGPT for core reaction mechanism weakens academic rigor; replace with peer-reviewed primary literature for depth and credibility.
Effective integration of quantitative literature data on ibuprofen photodegradation demonstrates coherent use of source material to contextualize the study.
The student interprets the concave trend by linking surface area and recombination effects, demonstrating coherent analysis aligned with observed data.
Appendix lists hazard codes, but main safety table lacks corresponding hazard details; integrate appendix with the primary safety table or add cross-references.
The control conditions for pH and stirring speed lack measurement details; specify how pH will be monitored and how stirring rate is standardized.
Missing values in the quantitative data table impede analysis validity; ensure complete data recording for each molar ratio trial.
Detailed presentation of absorbance changes and moles of ZnO permits clear calculation of degradation per mole, facilitating in-depth analysis.
Although error bars are mentioned, they are not visible; adjust graph formatting to display error bars clearly for accurate interpretation.
Qualitative observation on cloudiness hints at uneven light distribution; integrate this into data discussion to strengthen evaluation of experimental limitations.
The filter carry-over hypothesis is insightful but speculative; propose an experimental check (e.g., blank filtration test) to validate this error source.
The conclusion effectively links experimental findings to broader wastewater treatment applications, demonstrating critical reflection on real-world relevance.
Bibliographic entries show inconsistent formatting and include URLs without proper citation details; standardize to a consistent academic style (e.g., APA).
Inclusion of error propagation formulas in the appendix demonstrates thorough awareness of uncertainty analysis.
